prompt_text:
I'm going to describe two scenarios. In the memory scenario, a novel object called a clop has a property: it can be activated by pressing one of its buttons. Your job is to figure out which object in the perception scenario is the clop-analog, and therefore which button on it can be activated.

Memory scenario: There are three objects on a table: a clop, a moob, and a muk. The clop is underneath the moob. The moob is to-the-left-of the muk. The clop has a yellow button on top, a purple button on side, and a red button on bottom. The moob has a purple button on top and a yellow button on bottom. The muk has a red button on top and a yellow button on side. Pressing the yellow button on the clop activates it.

Perception scenario: There are three objects on a shelf: a nop, a glim, and a xav. The glim is underneath the xav. The xav is to-the-left-of the nop. The nop has a red button on top and a purple button on bottom. The glim has a yellow button on top, a purple button on side, and a red button on bottom. The xav has a purple button on top and a yellow button on side.

Which object in the perception scenario is the clop-analog, and which button activates it?
